issues.description: Missing type hints for entity parameter and return type. Inline import of MessageEntity unnecessary — can be added to top-level imports.
issues.fix_description: Added MessageEntity to top-level import from telegram. Added type hints: entity: MessageEntity, return -> MessageEntity. Removed inline import.

notes: file_sender.read_file_content (34 lines) and check_binary_file (26 lines) exceed 20-line guideline, but consist of sequential error handling blocks — refactoring would add indirection without clarity gain
_send_text_file (25 lines) is at the border but contains necessary loop+header logic
All public functions have docstrings, naming is clear and descriptive
No magic values — all constants are named (MAX_TEXT_FILE_SIZE_BYTES, TELEGRAM_MESSAGE_LIMIT, etc.)
No code duplication — _process_file_markers is shared between send_response and send_watcher_message

Path traversal: Claude can specify arbitrary paths in [SEND_FILE:] markers, but this is by design — Claude runs with full tool access on the user's machine, and the bot is single-user. No additional sandboxing needed.
Error messages include file paths (e.g. 'Файл не найден: /path/to/file') — acceptable since bot is single-user (user = machine owner) and messages are sent only to ALLOWED_USER_IDS.
Error messages sent with parse_mode=None — no HTML injection risk.
No user-controlled data is embedded in HTML-parsed messages in the new code paths.
Binary files go through check_binary_file (size check) before send_document — protects against accidentally sending huge files.
file_sender.py is a stateless utility module (like message_splitter) — no internal state, no imports from other project modules except telegram.MessageEntity
Dependency direction correct: bot.py (transport layer) calls file_sender (utility) — upper layer calls lower
No circular dependencies: file_sender does not import bot, process_manager, session_manager, etc.
New functions in bot.py are in the correct section (helper functions, before public API functions)
FILE_CONTENT_HEADER_TEMPLATE constant is in the constants section
All 7 functions from spec implemented in file_sender.py: extract_file_markers, strip_file_markers, is_text_file, read_file_content, render_file_for_telegram, convert_entities, check_binary_file
All 4 new functions in bot.py implemented: _process_file_markers, _send_text_file, _send_binary_file, _shift_entity
API signatures match spec exactly
Constants match spec: SEND_FILE_PATTERN, TEXT_EXTENSIONS, TEXT_FILE_NAMES, MAX_TEXT_FILE_SIZE_BYTES, MAX_BINARY_FILE_SIZE_BYTES, TELEGRAM_MESSAGE_LIMIT, EMPTY_FILE_PLACEHOLDER
Guard is_final=True implemented in both send_response and send_watcher_message per spec
File delivery order: files sent BEFORE text response (per architectural decision in spec)
Entities API used for text files (not parse_mode) per spec
requirements.txt and pyproject.toml updated with telegramify-markdown>=1.1.2 per spec
Error handling: all file errors return human-readable messages as strings (not exceptions) per spec
All 578 tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors)
Existing tests unaffected — text without [SEND_FILE:] markers passes through _process_file_markers unchanged (extract_file_markers returns empty list, function returns original text)
Guard is_final=True correctly prevents file marker processing for intermediate updates (is_final=False)
New test classes: TestSendResponseFileMarkers (5 tests), TestSendWatcherMessageFileMarkers (2 tests), TestProcessFileMarkers (1 test) in test_bot.py
New test classes in test_file_sender.py: 7 classes with 30 tests covering all public functions
New integration test TestMessagePathWithFileMarkers in test_message_path.py
Existing send_response tests (TestSendResponse) continue to pass — no regression in default behavior
